How It Works

From the MCC to a maintenance decision in five steps

Every SAM4 diagnostic starts in the motor control cabinet and ends as a validated maintenance alert. Between those points: edge processing, secure transmission, cloud AI, and expert review.

✓ No sensors on the asset ✓ No production shutdown ✓ No IT integration required
See the five-step pipeline ↓

Full data flow

Five steps from signal to insight

From capture to recommended action, the SAM4 pipeline is built for industrial environments. Five stages, fully automated, fully managed.

01

Capture at the MCC

Current transformers (CTs) and voltage taps are installed at the motor control cabinet. They capture the electrical signature — current and voltage waveforms — of every connected AC motor. Installation takes less than 60 minutes per machine. No production shutdown.

CT/VT clamps · Non-invasive · <60 min install
02

Edge processing & cellular transmission

The Samotics gateway samples electrical signals at up to 96 kHz, performs local signal conditioning, and transmits compressed data via built-in 4G/LTE cellular. No connection to your corporate network.

Up to 96 kHz sampling · 4G/LTE cellular · No IT dependency
03

Cloud analytics & AI

Electrical signatures are processed through AI models trained on thousands of industrial assets. The models detect anomalies, classify fault types, assess severity, and track degradation over time. New data continuously improves model accuracy.

AI-trained models · Fault classification · Continuous learning
04

Expert validation

Every AI-flagged anomaly is reviewed by a Samotics reliability engineer before an alert is sent. They confirm fault type, assess severity, write a specific recommended action, and estimate time to failure. Time-critical alerts are escalated immediately. Your team receives validated insights — not raw alerts to triage.

Engineer-validated · No false positives · Actionable recommendations
05

SAM4 dashboard & alerts

Validated diagnostics arrive in the SAM4 platform: asset health scores, fault alerts with severity and recommended actions, trend analysis, and fleet-level analytics. Alerts push to your CMMS via API integration so they reach the people who act on them.

Dashboard · Alerts · CMMS integration via API

Architecture

Built to pass your IT/OT security review

Traditional monitoring systems require corporate network access, on-premise servers, and ongoing IT support. SAM4 removes all three.

Every design choice below eliminates a common IT/OT blocker.

No corporate network required

Data transmits securely via built-in 4G/LTE.

✓ No corporate Wi-Fi ✓ No VPN ✓ No firewall configuration

Ethernet or Wi-Fi supported where preferred.

Complete OT network isolation

The gateway operates on a separate network path.

✓ No access to PLCs or SCADA ✓ One-way data flow ✓ No operational network exposure

Secure cloud architecture

Data processed and stored in secure cloud infrastructure.

✓ TLS 1.2+ encryption in transit ✓ AES-256 encryption at rest ✓ GDPR-compliant data residency

CMMS integration

Alerts integrate directly with maintenance systems.

✓ REST API ✓ SAP PM, IBM Maximo ✓ Custom dashboards

Minimal-downtime deployment

Sensors install at the MCC. Motor back online the same shift.

✓ Brief de-energisation only ✓ Standard LOTO procedure ✓ <60 minutes per machine

SAM4 operates independently from corporate IT infrastructure. No inbound connections to plant systems.

See the full technical architecture → View security architecture → See CMMS integrations →

“Because SAM4 installs inside the motor control cabinet, we were able to deploy it across a large number of assets at minimal cost.”

From your MCC to your dashboard in under 60 minutes

A 30-minute demo walks through the full pipeline — from signal capture to validated diagnostic — running on real industrial assets.
